spec_match.rationale: Accurately maps the async-safety race window and explains why the specification retired the implicit queueing mechanism.

interactive.rationale: The page is completely static, containing only a static grid timeline block and static code examples, violating the repo-wide interactivity rule.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Appropriately matches the core MSE async-deprecation blurb.

edge_cases.rationale: Lacks browser-support indicator or capability checks on load.

references.rationale: Mismatched ChromeStatus reference ID (uses 5141213094543360, which points to an unrelated entry).

design_system.rationale: Matches standard CSS and design variables.

openQuestions.title: Add interactive playback controls and timeline simulator
openQuestions.detail: The page is currently static, violating the repository rule that all concept pages must be genuinely interactive. Please add a step-by-step animator or playback controls to let the user visually trace the execution of commands, showing the exact moment the race opens up in pre-138 and when the exception throws in 138+.

openQuestions.title: Correct mismatched ChromeStatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: Line 96 contains `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5141213094543360`. Update it to the correct ID: `5190983595327488`.

summary: A highly descriptive sequence trace that details the exact timing window of the MSE queueing bug. However, the page violates the core interactivity rules because it is entirely static, and contains an incorrect, mismatched ChromeStatus link.
